comments.body: Thanks for your response. Unfortunately it throws the exact same error.
where are you specifying libs? in maven? or local.jar file?
I am specifying them in maven.
if possible can you attache that in your question?

I am not quite sure what you mean exactly. Let me just summarize how i did things so far. To add a library i opened the project settings in intellij. In the tab Libraries i clicked on the plus sign and copied the maven-repo-address into the corresponding field. Every address i added so far are already mentioned at the very end of my question. I am sorry if i did not understand you correctly. Java is giving me some trouble.

answers.body:
Is it possible for you to use

org.apache.httpcomponents httpclient 4.5.2

and then import org.apache.http.conn.ssl.SSLContexts
Try using "httpcore - 4.1.3" (of the same version as of your httpclient jar) jar file. You would be able to import org.apache.http.ssl.SSLContexts successfully.

This error may come even if you are not importing org.apache.http.ssl.SSLContexts

Try importing httpcore package from maven repository in pom.xml file.

Also check you have httpclient package.

It's not required to have the same version for httpcore and httpclient

title: Java org.apache.http.ssl.SSLContext not found
body:
I am trying to build a HttpClient but can't just figure out how to resolve this ClassNotFound-Error. I installed "org.apache.httpcomponents:httpclient:4.1.3" with maven and several other libraries but yet i won't work.

Code:

import org.apache.http.HttpEntity;
import org.apache.http.HttpResponse;
import org.apache.http.client.HttpClient;
import org.apache.http.client.config.CookieSpecs;
import org.apache.http.client.config.RequestConfig;
import org.apache.http.client.methods.HttpGet;
import org.apache.http.client.methods.HttpPost;
import org.apache.http.client.utils.URIBuilder;
import org.apache.http.entity.StringEntity;
import org.apache.http.impl.client.HttpClients;
import org.apache.http.util.EntityUtils;
import org.json.JSONArray;
import org.json.JSONObject;

import java.io.*;
import java.net.URISyntaxException;
import java.util.*;

public class Test {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        String bearerToken = "BLABLA";
        HttpClient httpClient = HttpClients.custom().setDefaultRequestConfig(RequestConfig.custom().setCookieSpec(CookieSpecs.STANDARD).build()).build();
    }
}

The corresponding error message:

Picked up _JAVA_OPTIONS: -Dawt.useSystemAAFontSettings=on -Dswing.aatext=true
Exception in thread "main" java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: org/apache/http/ssl/SSLContexts
    at org.apache.http.impl.client.HttpClientBuilder.build(HttpClientBuilder.java:977)
    at Twitter.main(Twitter.java:23)
Caused by: java.lang.ClassNotFoundException: org.apache.http.ssl.SSLContexts
    at java.base/jdk.internal.loader.BuiltinClassLoader.loadClass(BuiltinClassLoader.java:581)
    at java.base/jdk.internal.loader.ClassLoaders$AppClassLoader.loadClass(ClassLoaders.java:178)
    at java.base/java.lang.ClassLoader.loadClass(ClassLoader.java:522)

The maven repos i have installed currently:

oauth.signpost:signpost-commonshttp4:2.0.0
org.json:json:20200518
org.apache.httpcomponents:httpclient:4.1.3

Please note, that i am new to Java and Intellij-IDE.

I appreciate your help.
